sglang.title: [feature] [sgl-router] Add a dp-aware routing strategy
sglang.author: oldsharp
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR add an additional dp-aware routing strategy on top of the
sgl-router's hybrid "cache-aware load-balancing" routing strategy.
The strategy can be enabled by setting the --dp-aware flag when
starting the router.  When enabled, the router will try to contact the
workers to get the dp

sglang.title: [router] migrate router from actix to axum
sglang.author: slin1237
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR migrates the SGLang router from Actix-web to Axum, addressing several long-standing architectural limitations and improving the overall maintainability and performance of the router. The migration was necessary to:

Leverage Tower middleware ecosystem - Axum's integration with Tow

sglang.title: [Fix]Fix index oob in get_group_gemm_starts kernel.
sglang.author: HydraQYH
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Using int as offset or stride may sometimes cause OOB problems. For example, when M=128, N=8192, K=8192, and groups=256, executing the following code will cause OOB:
  
    
      sglang/sgl-kernel/csrc/moe/cutlass_moe_helper.cu
    
    
         Line 56
      in
      55ecdc0

Adding them here so that users can use sglang image out of the box on GKE.

sglang.title: [bugfix] Fix 2 minor bugs in the hicache storage layer
sglang.author: yapple
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This follows up a prior PR fixing synchronization bug: #8372

This PR fixes two issues in the hicache storage layer:
When a set operation fails, completed_token is 0. In this case, performing a split operation would result in an empty node, and subsequent Evict operations would hit an ass

sglang.title: fix incorrect increase of hit count
sglang.author: huangtingwei9988
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
hit count will incorrect increase in the following situations, which will cause hiradix cache to offload a large amount of worthless cache, leading to increased costs.

chunked_req, When a long input req is split into several chunks, each chunk will call cache_unfinished_req after prefill

sglang.title: Support l3 cache (mooncake store) for hiradix cache
sglang.author: huangtingwei9988
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Based on hiradix cache, we implemented L3 cache with mooncake store as the backend. For DeepSeek-R1 (tp=8) model, the 4k input hit L3 cache TTFT has been improved by nearly 50%.
Similar to LMCache, mooncake store can share kv cache across sglang instances
relate issue #6836
Modifications

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR adds support for providing multiple API keys for vllm server. Why is this important?
Supporting only one api key makes key rotation a big headache (besides the need to synchronize the update of downstream apps, vllm start time may take significant amount of time)

vllm.title: Reduce time wasted in GitHub Actions using `concurrency`
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
pre-commit and lint-and-deploy are both time consuming workflows which run on every commit in a given PR
This PR creates concurrency groups so that only the latest commit in a given PR has a running workflow for pre-commit and lint-and-deploy
pre-commit will still run for every commit on main
GitHub

This PR introduces support for running vLLM on TPUs via Pathways. The changes include the addition of a new environment variable, modifications to platform detection logic, and adjustments to multiprocessing behavior.
Pathways support:


Added a new environment variable, VLLM_TPU_USING_PATHWAYS, to

vllm.title: [Misc] Support more collective_rpc return types
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Because of how msgspec is used for the front-end <-> engine IPC message encoding, only "simple" native python data types/structures will correctly roundtrip when returned in the UtilityOutput struct from the call_utility(_async) method.
This change allows arbitrary msgspec-supported types to be retu

vllm.title: For VLLM_USE_PRECOMPILED, only compiled .so files should be extracted
vllm.author: dougbtv
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fixes up use of precompiled wheels so only compiled CUDA kernel .so files are extracted and used during the build. Prevents .py file pollution from the wheel, while still enabling downstream layers in the Dockerfile to install the wheel (for deps like torch).
Follow up to PR #20943
Re-used prior ext

vllm.author: cascade812

This PR adds torch async tp using compilation pass for scaled mm.
It builds upon previous work to extend async tensor parallelism support to quantized models.
It requires below config to run
config = CompilationConfig(
    level=3,
    compile_sizes=[4, 8, 16],
    splitting_ops=[],
)
config.pass_co

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Relax lang pin for voxtral
vllm.author: sanchit-gandhi
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Voxtral transcription works completely independently of whether the language token is specified. Currently, if the language token is not specified, it is forced to "en" silently, leading to unexpected results.
This PR:

Allows the language token to be None for Voxtral
Defaults to en for Whis
